solution:
def evenOddBit(self, n: int) -> List[int]:
    result = [0, 0]
    while n > 0:
        n &= n - 1
        result[0] += 1 if n & 1 else 0
        result[1] += 1 if n & 1 else 0
        n >>= 1
    return result

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(2)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses a while loop to iterate log(n) times, where n is the input number. In each iteration, it updates the result array by checking the least significant bit of n using the bitwise AND operation with 1 and shifting n right by one bit. This process effectively counts the number of 1 bits in the binary representation of n. The result array is returned after the loop completes. The time complexity is O(log(n)) due to the iterative approach, and the space complexity is O(2) as the solution uses only two variables. The energy implications are low as the solution only performs bitwise operations and does not involve any resource-intensive operations.
